description:
You are an incident-response analyst who has been handed a small evidence bundle under
/home/user/ir_case_41.

Content already present when the session starts
----------------------------------------------
1. /home/user/ir_case_41/login_events.json  
   • An array of eight JSON objects – each describing one interactive
     login attempt.  
2. /home/user/ir_case_41/login_schema.json  
   • A JSON-Schema (Draft-07) that documents how every *legitimate*
     login event **must** look.  
   • High-level rules (you do **NOT** have to re-implement them, they are
     already encoded in the file):  
     – Required properties: timestamp, username, src_ip, auth_result  
     – timestamp must be an ISO-8601 string (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`)  
     – src_ip must match a valid IPv4 dotted-quad  
     – auth_result is an enum whose value is either “SUCCESS” or “FAILURE”  
     – No additional properties are permitted

Your investigation goals
------------------------
A. Validate every event in login_events.json against login_schema.json.  
B. Isolate every event that *fails* validation (call these “suspicious
   events”).  
C. Generate three artefacts summarising your findings:

   1. /home/user/ir_case_41/validation_summary.json  
      • MUST be **exactly** the following canonical form  
        (pretty-printed with 2-space indentation, no trailing spaces).  

        {
          "total_events": <integer>,
          "valid_events": <integer>,
          "invalid_events": <integer>,
          "unique_usernames_valid": [<string>, …],
          "invalid_reasons_count": {
            "timestamp_format": <integer>,
            "missing_required": <integer>,
            "pattern_mismatch": <integer>,
            "additional_properties": <integer>
          }
        }

      • Keys must appear in the order shown above.  
      • The array unique_usernames_valid must be alphabetically sorted
        (ascending) and contain *only* usernames from events that pass
        validation.

   2. /home/user/ir_case_41/suspicious_logins.json  
      • MUST contain **only** the events that fail validation, preserved
        byte-for-byte as they appeared in login_events.json.  
      • MUST be an array, pretty-printed with 2-space indentation.  
      • Events must be ordered by their timestamp **ascending**.  
        (If an event’s timestamp is malformed, treat its raw value as a
        string during the sort; ordinary lexicographic order is enough.)

   3. /home/user/ir_case_41/suspicious_logins.log  
      • Human-readable log file, one line per invalid event.  
      • EXACT format for each line:  

        <timestamp> - <issue_description>

        Examples:  
        2023-08-25T03:19:00Z - Missing required field: username  
        2023-08-25T03:20:00Z - src_ip pattern mismatch  

      • Lines must be sorted in the same order used for
        suspicious_logins.json and terminated with ‘\n’.  
      • issue_description must precisely describe the first validation
        error encountered for that event, using one of the four phrasings
        that also appear as object keys in invalid_reasons_count:  
          ▸ Invalid timestamp format  
          ▸ Missing required field: <fieldname>  
          ▸ src_ip pattern mismatch  
          ▸ Additional property not allowed: <propertyname>

Constraints & tools
-------------------
• All work must be done as the unprivileged user `user`.  
• Only files inside /home/user/ir_case_41 are relevant; do *not* create
  artefacts elsewhere.  
• Feel free to use any combination of jq, python3 + jsonschema, awk,
  grep, etc. Parallel and pipeline strategies are welcome, but the
  resulting files **must** exactly match the formats above.

Success criteria (checked automatically)
----------------------------------------
1. validation_summary.json exists, is valid JSON, and matches the exact
   structure and numeric values expected for the supplied evidence.  
2. suspicious_logins.json exists, contains *only* the invalid events, in
   ascending timestamp order, pretty-printed.  
3. suspicious_logins.log exists and each line follows the enforced
   template, correctly describing the validation error, in the correct
   order.  
4. No extraneous files were added to /home/user/ir_case_41.

Create the three artefacts exactly as specified and you will have
completed the task.
